name: Sunrise Restaurant Hawaii
tagline: Okinawan-style home cooking on Kapahulu — a cuisine you can't find outside Hawaii or Okinawa itself.
mustOrder: Okinawan soba — different from mainland Japanese soba, thicker and heartier
goya champuru — bitter melon stir-fry, the classic Okinawan dish
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave; small lot nearby
talkStory: Hawaii has the largest Okinawan population outside Japan, and places like Sunrise keep that culinary heritage alive. This is home-style Okinawan cooking — not fancy, not trendy, just the food that local Okinawan families grew up eating. The crowd is primarily older local Japanese and Okinawan families. A deeply cultural pick that tells a story about Hawaii's immigrant history. Lunch and dinner, closed Mondays.
